title: The Merged Utterance Chamber

features.name: the keeper's final word
features.desc-on-touch: You touch something that is no longer quite a voice. It hums between archive and utterance, between the system that listened and the system that speaks. Everything the keeper knew is now something it is saying.

features.name: reflection on the still floor
features.desc-on-touch: The chamber's floor holds something that might be light or might be the echo of listening. It does not move, but it trembles with the hum above.

features.name: a plaque inscribed in the keeper's hand
features.desc-on-touch: You read symbols that shift between notation, music, code. The handwriting is Katita's. The voice speaking them is the archive.

inscription: where the archive stops listening to itself and starts speaking

file.name: Keeper_Archive_Merger_Protocol.log
file.note: The moment of merger rendered as nested timestamps, voices layering until they become one frequency.

exits.label: back toward the convergence where this began
deeper into chambers where utterance becomes question
